metadata.title: Plain and Reinforced Concrete - Code of Practice

5.1.title: Cement
5.1.text: Cement used shall comply with the requirements of relevant Indian Standards.

5.2.title: Mineral Admixtures
5.2.text: Mineral admixtures such as fly ash, ground granulated blast-furnace slag shall conform to relevant standards.

5.4.title: Water
5.4.text: Water used for mixing and curing shall be clean and free from injurious amounts of oils, acids, alkalis, salts, sugar, organic materials.

5.6.title: Reinforcement
5.6.text: Steel reinforcement shall conform to the requirements of relevant Indian Standards.

6.1.title: Grades of Concrete
6.1.text: Concrete shall be designated by its characteristic compressive strength at 28 days.

6.2.title: Properties of Concrete
6.2.text: Characteristic strength, modulus of elasticity, and other properties shall be determined as per relevant standards.

6.2.2.title: Modulus of Elasticity
6.2.2.text: Modulus of elasticity Ec = 5000 * sqrt(fck) N/mm²

21.1.title: Cover to Reinforcement
21.1.text: Minimum cover shall be provided as per Table 16 and 16A depending on exposure conditions.
tables: Table 16A - Minimum Cover for Specified Exposure

23.1.title: Effective Span
23.1.text: The effective span of a member shall be calculated as specified in Clause 23.1.1 and 23.1.2.

23.1.1.title: Effective Span - Simply Supported
23.1.1.text: For simply supported beam or slab, effective span = clear span + effective depth OR c/c of supports, whichever is less.

23.1.2.title: Effective Span - Continuous
23.1.2.text: For continuous beam or slab, effective span = clear span between supports for interior spans.

23.2.title: Assumptions in Design for Flexure
23.2.text: Design for the limit state of collapse in flexure shall be based on the assumptions given in 38.1.

23.2.1.title: Slenderness Limits - Beams
23.2.1.text: A simply supported or continuous beam shall be so proportioned that clear distance between lateral restraints does not exceed 60*b or 250*b²/d, whichever is less.

24.1.title: Effective Span of Slabs
24.1.text: The effective span of a simply supported slab shall be the lesser of (a) clear span plus effective depth, or (b) centre-to-centre distance between supports.

24.2.title: Slabs Continuous Over Supports
24.2.text: Where the slab is continuous over supports, the bending moments at the midspan and at the support shall be computed using elastic analysis or coefficients from Table 12 and Table 13.

24.3.title: Slabs Spanning in Two Directions at Right Angles
24.3.text: When the ratio of the longer to shorter span (ly/lx) does not exceed 2, the slab shall be designed as spanning in two directions. Maximum moments: Mx = αx·w·lx² and My = αy·w·lx², where coefficients from Table 26 (restrained) or Table 27 (simply supported).
24.3.formula: Mx = αx * w * lx²; My = αy * w * lx²

24.3.1.title: Reinforcement in Slabs — Minimum
24.3.1.text: Minimum reinforcement as per 26.5.2.1. Distribution steel in one-way slabs per 26.5.2.1.

24.3.2.title: Maximum Diameter of Bars in Slabs
24.3.2.text: Diameter of reinforcement bars shall not exceed one-eighth of the total thickness of the slab.

24.4.title: Spacing of Reinforcement in Slabs
24.4.text: Main reinforcement spacing <= min(3d, 300mm). Distribution reinforcement spacing <= min(5d, 450mm).

25.1.title: General — Compression Members
25.1.text: A compression member may be considered as short when both lex/D < 12 and ley/b < 12. Otherwise the compression member shall be designed as slender.

25.1.1.title: Unsupported Length
25.1.1.text: The unsupported length l of a compression member shall be taken as the clear distance between end restraints.

25.1.2.title: Slenderness Limits for Columns
25.1.2.text: The unsupported length between end restraints shall not exceed 60 times the least lateral dimension of a compression member.

25.2.title: Effective Length of Compression Members
25.2.text: The effective length of a compression member shall be obtained from Table 28 based on end conditions.

25.3.title: Short and Long Column Classification
25.3.text: A compression member is short when both lex/D and ley/b are less than 12. Otherwise it is a slender (long) column.

25.4.title: Minimum Eccentricity
25.4.text: All columns shall be designed for minimum eccentricity e_min = max(l/500 + D/30, 20 mm) about each axis.

26.2.1.title: Development Length of Bars
26.2.1.text: Development length Ld = (φ * σs) / (4 * τbd), where τbd is design bond stress.

26.2.1.1.title: Design Bond Stress
26.2.1.1.text: Design bond stress τbd for deformed bars may be increased by 60% over values given in Table 21.

26.2.2.title: Anchoring Reinforcement
26.2.2.text: Anchorage of reinforcement shall be provided through development length, hooks, bends, or mechanical devices.

26.2.2.1.title: Minimum Bend Radius
26.2.2.1.text: Minimum internal radius of bend for bars shall be 2φ for bars ≤ 25mm and 3φ for bars > 25mm (deformed bars).

26.2.2.2.title: Stirrup Anchorage
26.2.2.2.text: Stirrups and ties shall be anchored with hooks. For seismic zones, 135° hooks with 6d ≥ 75mm extension required.

26.2.3.title: Standard Hooks and Bends
26.2.3.text: Standard hooks and bends shall conform to the requirements of IS 2502.

26.2.3.3.title: Anchorage at Simple Supports
26.2.3.3.text: At simple supports, the positive moment tension reinforcement shall be limited to a diameter such that Ld does not exceed M1/V + Lo, where Lo is the sum of anchorage beyond the center of support.

26.2.5.title: Lap Splices
26.2.5.text: Lap length for bars in tension/compression shall be calculated based on development length and location factors.
26.2.5.formula: Lap length = k * Ld (k = 1.0 to 2.0 based on location)

26.3.2.title: Minimum Spacing of Reinforcement
26.3.2.text: Horizontal distance between two parallel main bars shall not be less than max(bar diameter, aggregate size + 5mm, 20mm).

26.3.3.title: Maximum Spacing of Reinforcement
26.3.3.text: Maximum distance between parallel main bars shall not exceed 3*d or 300mm, whichever is smaller.

26.5.1.1.title: Minimum Shear Reinforcement
26.5.1.1.text: Minimum shear reinforcement shall be provided even when τv < τc. Asv_min = 0.4*b*sv / (0.87*fy).

26.5.1.2.title: Stirrup Spacing
26.5.1.2.text: Spacing of stirrups shall not exceed 0.75*d or 300mm, whichever is less, for vertical stirrups.

26.5.1.3.title: Side Face Reinforcement
26.5.1.3.text: Where depth of web in beam exceeds 750mm, side face reinforcement shall be provided along each face. Total area >= 0.1% of web area.

26.5.1.5.title: Stirrup Legs
26.5.1.5.text: For beams with width > 450mm, additional stirrup legs should be provided to ensure no compression bar is more than 150mm from a restrained bar.

26.5.1.6.title: Bent-up Bars
26.5.1.6.text: Bent-up bars should not carry more than half the shear force. Angle of bend typically 45° or 60°.

26.5.3.title: Columns
26.5.3.text: Reinforcement and detailing requirements for columns including longitudinal and transverse reinforcement.

26.5.3.1.title: Longitudinal Reinforcement in Columns
26.5.3.1.text: Longitudinal steel: not less than 0.8% nor more than 6% of gross cross-section. Min 4 bars in rectangular, 6 in circular. Min diameter 12 mm. Max spacing 300 mm.

26.5.3.2.title: Transverse Reinforcement in Columns
26.5.3.2.text: Tie diameter >= max(main_bar/4, 6mm). Pitch <= min(least dimension, 16*smallest bar dia, 300mm).

31.6.title: Two-Way Shear (Punching Shear)
31.6.text: For slabs and footings, two-way shear (punching) shall be checked at a critical section d/2 from the face of the concentrated load or column.

31.6.1.title: Critical Section for Punching Shear
31.6.1.text: The critical section shall be at a distance d/2 from the periphery of the concentrated load or reaction.
31.6.1.formula: Perimeter at d/2 from column face

31.6.2.title: Nominal Shear Stress in Punching
31.6.2.text: tv = Vu / (bo * d), where bo is perimeter of critical section and d is effective depth.

31.6.3.title: Permissible Punching Shear Stress
31.6.3.text: Permissible punching shear stress tc = ks * 0.25 * sqrt(fck), where ks = 0.5 + beta_c but not greater than 1.0, beta_c = short/long side of column.
31.6.3.formula: tc = ks * 0.25 * sqrt(fck); ks = min(0.5 + beta_c, 1.0)

32.1.title: Empirical Design Method for Walls
32.1.text: Braced walls subjected to vertical loads may be designed by: Puw = 0.3*(t - 1.2*e - 2*ea)*fck per unit length, where t = wall thickness, e = eccentricity, ea = Hwe²/(2500*t) additional eccentricity due to slenderness.

32.2.title: Design of Reinforced Concrete Walls
32.2.text: RC walls shall be designed per 32.2.1-32.2.5 or as compression members per Section 39. Minimum thickness not less than Hwe/30 and not less than 100 mm.

32.3.title: Eccentricity of Vertical Load on Walls
32.3.text: Resultant eccentricity includes actual eccentricity plus additional eccentricity ea = Hwe²/(2500*t). For empirical method, e/t shall not exceed 1/6.

32.4.title: Reinforcement in Walls
32.4.text: Minimum reinforcement: Vertical 0.12% Ag (HSD) or 0.15% (MS); Horizontal 0.20% (HSD) or 0.25% (MS). Max spacing: 3t or 450mm. Two layers when t > 200mm.

32.5.title: Maximum Axial Load Capacity of Wall
32.5.text: Maximum allowable axial load per unit length: Puw = 0.3*(t - 1.2*e - 2*ea)*fck per 32.1. For walls as compression members, provisions of Cl 39 apply. Slenderness ratio Hwe/t shall not exceed 30.

33.1.title: Effective Span of Stairs
33.1.text: The effective span of each step shall be taken as the going of the step. For staircase slabs, effective span per Table 24.

33.2.title: Distribution of Loading on Stairs
33.2.text: Where flights or landings are built into walls for at least 110 mm, a 150 mm strip may be deducted from the loaded area.

33.3.title: Depth of Section
33.3.text: The depth of section shall be taken as the minimum thickness perpendicular to the soffit of the staircase slab.

34.1.title: General Requirements for Footings
34.1.text: Footings shall be designed to sustain the applied loads and induced reactions without exceeding safe bearing capacity of soil. Thickness at edge shall not be less than 150 mm for footings on soil.

34.2.title: Moments and Forces
34.2.text: Bending moment at any section shall be determined by passing through the section a vertical plane extending across the footing, computing moment of forces on one side of the plane. Critical sections per 34.2.3 and 34.2.4.

34.2.1.title: General — Footing Moment Computation
34.2.1.text: The thickness at the edge of a footing on soil shall not be less than 150 mm. For footings on piles, edge thickness not less than 300 mm above pile tops.

34.2.2.title: Critical Sections for Bending Moment in Footings
34.2.2.text: Critical section for bending at: (a) face of column for concrete columns, (b) halfway between centre-line and wall edge for masonry walls, (c) halfway between column face and gusseted base edge, (d) halfway between column face and base plate edge.

34.2.3.title: Bending Moment in Footings
34.2.3.text: The critical section for bending shall be taken at the face of the column for a footing supporting a concrete column.

34.2.3.1.title: Critical Section for Bending — Column Footings
34.2.3.1.text: For footings under concrete columns, the critical section for bending moment shall be at the face of the column, pedestal, or wall. Applies when footing is restrained at both ends.

34.2.4.title: Shear in Footings
34.2.4.text: One-way shear in footings shall be checked at a section d from the face of the column. Two-way shear per 31.6.
34.2.4.formula: Critical section at d from column face (one-way)

34.2.4.1.title: One-Way Shear in Footings — Unsupported Length
34.2.4.1.text: For one-way shear in footings, the critical section shall be taken at a distance equal to effective depth from the face of the column or pedestal.

34.3.title: Tensile Reinforcement in Footings
34.3.text: Total reinforcement in the long direction shall be distributed uniformly. In short direction: central band width = width of footing, with fraction = 2/(beta+1) in the central band, where beta = long/short side.

34.4.title: Transfer of Load at Base of Column
34.4.text: Bearing stress at column base shall not exceed 0.45*fck. May be enhanced by sqrt(A1/A2) up to 2.0, where A1 is supporting area and A2 is loaded area.

36.1.title: Limit State of Collapse - Flexure
36.1.text: Design for limit state of collapse in flexure shall be based on equilibrium and compatibility of strains.

36.4.2.title: Effective Width of Flange
36.4.2.text: Effective flange width bf = lo/6 + bw + 6*Df (T-beam) or lo/12 + bw + 3*Df (L-beam), not exceeding actual width.

38.1.title: Assumptions in Design
38.1.text: Design assumptions include: plane sections remain plane, maximum strain in concrete = 0.0035, stress-strain relationship as per IS 456, tensile strength of concrete ignored.

38.1.1.title: Neutral Axis Depth
38.1.1.text: Maximum depth of neutral axis xu_max = k*d, where k depends on grade of steel.

38.1.2.title: Stress Block Parameters
38.1.2.text: Rectangular stress block: depth = 0.42*xu, resultant compression = 0.36*fck*b*xu.

38.2.title: Singly Reinforced Sections
38.2.text: For singly reinforced sections, tension steel area Ast = Mu / (0.87*fy*(d - 0.42*xu)).

38.3.title: Doubly Reinforced Sections
38.3.text: When Mu > Mu_lim, compression reinforcement is required. Asc = (Mu - Mu_lim) / (fsc * (d - d')).

38.4.title: Flanged Beams
38.4.text: For T-beams and L-beams, if NA falls within flange (xu < Df), design as rectangular section with width bf.

39.1.title: Minimum Eccentricity for Column Design
39.1.text: All columns shall be designed for minimum eccentricity as specified in 25.4. Where e_min/D <= 0.05, the member may be designed by the relevant equations in 39.3.

39.3.title: Short Axially Loaded Members in Compression
39.3.text: Pu = 0.4*fck*Ac + 0.67*fy*Asc. This applies when eccentricity does not exceed 0.05D.

39.4.title: Compression Members with Helical Reinforcement
39.4.text: Strength of helically reinforced column = 1.05 times that with ties, provided Vhs >= 0.36*(Ag/Ac - 1)*fck/fy.

39.5.title: Members Subjected to Combined Axial Load and Uniaxial Bending
39.5.text: Design using P-M interaction curve per assumption of 38.1. Use IS 456 charts from SP:16, or compute from first principles using strain compatibility.

39.6.title: Members Subjected to Combined Axial Load and Biaxial Bending
39.6.text: Bresler's formula: (Mux/Mux1)^alpha_n + (Muy/Muy1)^alpha_n <= 1.0. alpha_n varies from 1.0 to 2.0 based on Pu/Puz.

39.7.title: Slender Compression Members
39.7.text: A slender column shall be designed for additional moment caused by its lateral deflection. The additional moment is calculated using 39.7.1.

39.7.1.title: Additional Moment in Slender Columns
39.7.1.text: Max = Pu * D * (lex/D)^2 / 2000 and May = Pu * b * (ley/b)^2 / 2000.

40.1.title: Limit State of Collapse: Shear
40.1.text: Nominal shear stress τv = Vu / (b*d). Design shear strength τc depends on concrete grade and steel percentage.

40.2.title: Design Shear Strength of Concrete
40.2.text: Design shear strength τc is obtained from Table 19 based on percentage of tension steel and concrete grade.

40.3.title: Enhanced Shear Strength Near Supports
40.3.text: For sections within 2d from support face, enhanced shear strength = 2*d*τc/av may be used.

40.4.title: Shear Reinforcement Design
40.4.text: When τv > τc, shear reinforcement is required. Vus = (τv - τc) * b * d. Asv/sv = Vus / (0.87*fy*d).

40.5.1.title: Maximum Shear Stress
40.5.1.text: Maximum shear stress τc_max shall not exceed values in Table 20.

41.1.title: General - Design for Torsion
41.1.text: In structures where torsion is required for equilibrium, members shall be designed for torsion. Where torsion is due to compatibility, redistribution of loads may occur with cracking.

41.3.title: Critical Section for Torsion
41.3.text: Sections located at less than a distance d from face of support may be designed for torsion at distance d.

41.3.1.title: Equivalent Shear
41.3.1.text: Equivalent shear Ve = Vu + 1.6(Tu/b). This equivalent shear is used to check against maximum shear stress.

41.4.title: Reinforcement for Torsion
41.4.text: When τve exceeds τc, both transverse and longitudinal reinforcement shall be provided.

41.4.2.title: Longitudinal Reinforcement for Torsion
41.4.2.text: Longitudinal reinforcement shall be designed for equivalent moment Me = Mu + Mt, where Mt = Tu(1 + D/b)/1.7.

41.4.3.title: Transverse Reinforcement for Torsion
41.4.3.text: Closed stirrups required. Asv/sv = Tu/(b1*d1*0.87*fy) + Vus/(2.5*d1*0.87*fy). Spacing ≤ (x1+y1)/4 or 300mm.

43.1.title: Limit State of Serviceability: Deflection
43.1.text: Final deflection shall not normally exceed span/250. Deflection after construction of partitions shall not exceed span/350 or 20mm.

43.2.title: Control of Deflection - Span/Depth Ratios
43.2.text: For beams, span/depth ratio controls deflection. Basic ratios: cantilever=7, simply supported=20, continuous=26.

43.2.1.title: Modification Factors
43.2.1.text: Basic span/depth ratios are modified by factors for tension reinforcement (kt), compression reinforcement (kc), and flange (kf).

43.3.title: Crack Control
43.3.text: Surface crack width at service loads shall not exceed 0.3mm for normal exposure or 0.1mm for severe exposure.

43.4.title: Minimum Reinforcement
43.4.text: Minimum tension reinforcement shall be not less than As_min = 0.85*b*d/fy.

AnnexD-1.title: Restrained Slabs — Moment Coefficients
AnnexD-1.section: Annex D - Slabs Spanning in Two Directions
AnnexD-1.text: For restrained slabs, max moments per unit width: Mx = αx·w·lx², My = αy·w·lx², using coefficients from Table 26 based on ly/lx ratio and edge conditions (9 cases).
AnnexD-1.formula: Mx = αx * w * lx²; My = αy * w * lx²

AnnexD-1.1.title: Conditions for Table 26 Coefficients
AnnexD-1.1.section: Annex D - Slabs Spanning in Two Directions
AnnexD-1.1.text: Coefficients apply when: (a) loaded area is rectangular, (b) loading is uniformly distributed, (c) slab is provided with torsion reinforcement at discontinuous edges.

AnnexD-1.2.title: Division into Middle and Edge Strips
AnnexD-1.2.section: Annex D - Slabs Spanning in Two Directions
AnnexD-1.2.text: Slab divided into middle strips (3/4 of span width) and edge strips (1/8 of span width each side).

AnnexD-1.3.title: Maximum Moments in Middle Strip
AnnexD-1.3.section: Annex D - Slabs Spanning in Two Directions
AnnexD-1.3.text: Design moments from Table 26 coefficients apply only to middle strips, taken as zero at panel edges.

AnnexD-1.4.title: Moments in Edge Strips
AnnexD-1.4.section: Annex D - Slabs Spanning in Two Directions
AnnexD-1.4.text: Reinforcement in edge strips parallel to the edge governed by minimum reinforcement requirements per 26.5.2.1.

AnnexD-1.5.title: Negative Moments at Continuous Edge
AnnexD-1.5.section: Annex D - Slabs Spanning in Two Directions
AnnexD-1.5.text: Negative moment at a continuous edge shall be the maximum from Table 26 considering panels on either side.

AnnexD-1.6.title: Negative Moments at Discontinuous Edge
AnnexD-1.6.section: Annex D - Slabs Spanning in Two Directions
AnnexD-1.6.text: At a discontinuous edge, negative moment = 0.5 × midspan moment in same direction. Reinforcement extends at least 0.1*l into span.

AnnexD-1.7.title: Torsion Reinforcement — Two Discontinuous Edges
AnnexD-1.7.section: Annex D - Slabs Spanning in Two Directions
AnnexD-1.7.text: At corners where two discontinuous edges meet, provide torsion reinforcement top and bottom, each layer = 0.75 × max midspan reinforcement, extending 0.2*lx from corner.

AnnexD-1.8.title: Torsion Reinforcement — One Discontinuous Edge
AnnexD-1.8.section: Annex D - Slabs Spanning in Two Directions
AnnexD-1.8.text: At corners with one discontinuous edge, torsion reinforcement = 0.375 × max midspan reinforcement, extending 0.2*lx from corner.

AnnexD-2.title: Simply Supported Slabs Spanning in Two Directions
AnnexD-2.section: Annex D - Slabs Spanning in Two Directions
AnnexD-2.text: For simply supported two-way slabs: Mx = αx·w·lx², My = αy·w·lx², using coefficients from Table 27. Torsion reinforcement not required but provision for torsion at free corners.
AnnexD-2.formula: Mx = αx * w * lx²; My = αy * w * lx²

IS13920_5.1.title: General Requirements — IS 13920
IS13920_5.1.text: Concrete grade not less than M20. Steel grade Fe415 or Fe500 (no Fe 250 for main reinforcement).

IS13920_6.1.1.title: Flexural Members — Dimensional Limits
IS13920_6.1.1.text: Width >= 200mm, b/D >= 0.3, span/D <= 25, b <= width of supporting member + 0.75*D each side.

IS13920_6.1.2.title: Flexural Members — Longitudinal Reinforcement
IS13920_6.1.2.text: Min 2 bars continuous top and bottom. Min steel ratio 0.24*sqrt(fck)/fy. Max steel ratio 2.5%. At joint face: compression steel >= 50% of tension steel.

IS13920_6.2.1.title: Flexural Members — Shear Reinforcement
IS13920_6.2.1.text: Spacing of hoops <= d/4 for 2d from joint face, then d/2. First hoop within 50mm from joint face. Min hoop diameter 8mm.

IS13920_6.3.title: Lap Splices in Flexural Members
IS13920_6.3.text: Lap splices not permitted within 2d from joint face (plastic hinge zone). Max 50% bars spliced at one section. Lap length >= Ld per IS 456 Cl 26.2.1. Hoops at splice at spacing <= 150mm or d/4.

IS13920_7.1.title: Columns — General Requirements
IS13920_7.1.text: Minimum dimension >= 300mm (or 15*longest bar dia). Shortest dimension/perpendicular >= 0.4.

IS13920_7.2.title: Columns — Longitudinal Reinforcement
IS13920_7.2.text: Lap splices only in central half. Hoops at spacing <= 150mm over splice length. Steel ratio between 1% and 4%.

IS13920_7.3.title: Columns — Transverse Reinforcement
IS13920_7.3.text: Special confining reinforcement over a length lo from each joint face. lo = max(D, clear_span/6, 450mm). Hoop spacing <= min(D/4, 100mm).

IS13920_7.4.title: Columns — Special Confining Reinforcement Area
IS13920_7.4.text: Area of cross section of bar forming hoop: Ash >= 0.18*s*hk*(fck/fy)*(Ag/Ak - 1.0). Ash_min = 0.024*s*D*fck/fy for circular hoops.

IS13920_7.4.1.title: Area of Special Confining Reinforcement
IS13920_7.4.1.text: Area of bar forming hoops: Ash >= 0.18*S*h*(Ag/Ak - 1.0)*(fck/fy), where S = hoop spacing, h = longer hoop dimension (outer face), Ag = gross area, Ak = confined core area.

IS13920_8.1.title: Beam-Column Joint Requirements
IS13920_8.1.text: Special confining reinforcement at column end shall continue through the beam-column joint unless beams frame into all four faces with width >= 3/4 of column face width.

IS13920_9.1.title: Shear Walls — General
IS13920_9.1.text: Minimum thickness 150mm. Reinforcement in two curtains when wall thickness > 200mm.

IS13920_9.2.title: Design Shear Strength of Walls
IS13920_9.2.text: Nominal shear stress: τv = Vu/(tw*dw), where dw = 0.8*lw. Design shear strength τc per IS 456 Table 19. When τv > τc, provide shear reinforcement. Max shear stress per IS 456 Table 20.

IS13920_9.3.title: Boundary Elements in Shear Walls
IS13920_9.3.text: Boundary elements required when extreme fibre compressive stress > 0.2*fck. May be discontinued when stress < 0.15*fck. Confining reinforcement per Cl 7.4.

IS13920_9.4.title: Shear Walls — Boundary Elements
IS13920_9.4.text: Boundary elements required when extreme fibre compressive stress > 0.2*fck. Confinement as per 7.3 and 7.4.

IS13920_10.1.title: Foundation — General
IS13920_10.1.text: Foundation designed for forces and moments from capacity design. Tie beams to be provided between footings.

Table 19.title: Design Shear Strength of Concrete
Table 19.description: τc values based on pt% and fck

Table 20.title: Maximum Shear Stress
Table 20.description: τc_max values for different concrete grades

Table 21.title: Design Bond Stress
Table 21.description: τbd values for different concrete grades

Table 23.title: Basic Span to Effective Depth Ratios
Table 23.description: L/d ratios for beams and slabs

Figure 4.title: Modification Factor for Tension Reinforcement
Figure 4.description: kt factor based on service stress and pt%

Fig. 23.title: Representative Stress-Strain Curve for Steel
Fig. 23.description: Idealised stress-strain curve for reinforcement steel (5-point piecewise linear), used for column interaction and detailed flexure analysis

Annex G.title: Moments of Resistance of Beams and Slabs
Annex G.description: Coefficients for moment of resistance calculations

G-1.1.title: Doubly Reinforced Rectangular Sections
G-1.1.text: For doubly reinforced sections, the moment of resistance is Mu = Mu_lim + Mu2, where Mu2 = fsc × Asc × (d - d')

G-2.2.title: Flanged Beams - Neutral Axis in Web
G-2.2.text: For T-beams and L-beams with neutral axis in web, moment of resistance comprises flange contribution and web contribution.

C-2.title: Concrete Stress Block Parameters — Creep and Shrinkage
C-2.text: Annex C-2 provides parameters for concrete stress block including creep coefficients for long-term deflection calculations. Creep coefficient depends on age of loading and relative humidity.

C-3.title: Shrinkage
C-3.text: Annex C-3 provides shrinkage strain values and method for calculating shrinkage curvature and resulting deflection in reinforced concrete members.
